model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With only 256 training steps, the baseline is likely under-optimized. A higher learning rate should improve early progress, while AdamW with slightly reduced weight decay keeps regularization but avoids over-penalizing in this short-budget regime.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"    total_steps = steps_per_epoch * 100","new":"    total_steps = TRAIN_MAX_STEPS if TRAIN_MAX_STEPS is not None else steps_per_epoch * 100","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"Under a fixed 256-step budget, applying cosine decay across the actual training horizon should improve final validation loss versus a constant high LR by reducing late-step over-updates while keeping fast early learning."}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","secondary_modes":["summaries"],"rationale":"The cosine schedule over this short horizon hurt performance, so reverting to constant LR restores stronger early optimization. Adding mild label smoothing should improve calibration and validation loss without adding architectural complexity."}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":["caching"],"rationale":"Recent feedback suggests the run is sensitive to over-regularization and late-step instability. Removing label smoothing and slightly lowering LR should preserve strong early AdamW progress while reducing overshoot, targeting lower validation loss within the same 256-step budget."}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"Current results suggest optimization is not the main bottleneck now. Increasing feature and head capacity should improve representational power for CIFAR-10 within 256 steps, while restoring the stronger 1e-3 AdamW learning rate keeps fast convergence on the larger model."}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The larger model at 16 channels already helped substantially, suggesting capacity is still the limiting factor. Increasing base channels further while slightly reducing LR should improve representational power without destabilizing short-budget optimization."}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DEPTH = 2","new":"DEPTH = 3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"Validation loss is still improving with added capacity, and there is remaining budget headroom. Increasing depth adds another convolutional stage (more hierarchy and nonlinearity) while keeping the script simple and often improves CIFAR-10 loss more than further width-only scaling."}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DEPTH = 3","new":"DEPTH = 2","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-5","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","secondary_modes":["caching"],"rationale":"The added depth clearly over-shot the fixed-step budget, so reverting to depth 2 restores the stronger training regime. With the wider model already helping, a slightly lower weight decay can reduce underfitting and potentially lower validation loss further."}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-5","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","secondary_modes":["caching"],"rationale":"The reduced weight decay regressed, so restore the stronger 5e-5 setting. Then increase classifier width to 96 to add head capacity with minimal complexity, which can improve class separation under the same fixed-step budget."}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The last capacity increase in the classifier head improved validation loss, so a further modest head-width increase is a low-risk continuation of that trend with minimal code complexity and no optimizer changes."}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","secondary_modes":["layout"],"rationale":"The larger head regressed, so revert to the stronger 96 setting. Then increase batch size to process substantially more training examples within the same fixed 256-step budget, which can improve validation loss in this step-limited regime."}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 192","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The large jump from batch 64 to 128 indicates step-limited training benefits strongly from more examples per update. Increasing to 192 should further improve sample coverage within 256 steps, and a modest LR increase helps maintain update magnitude with the larger batch."}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 192","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"Performance has improved strongly as batch size increased, consistent with a step-limited regime where more examples per step helps most. Pushing to batch 256 and scaling LR up modestly should preserve optimization speed while increasing effective data coverage within 256 updates."}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 192","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","secondary_modes":["micro","topk"],"rationale":"The 256/1.2e-3 setting regressed, so return to the stronger 192/1e-3 regime. Then lower AdamW beta2 to 0.99 to make second-moment adaptation more responsive in this short-horizon training, which can improve convergence and validation loss within 256 steps."}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 9e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":["indexing"],"rationale":"Lowering beta2 did not improve results, so restore the more stable AdamW moment setting. Then reduce LR slightly from 1e-3 to 9e-4 to cut late-step overshoot while keeping most of the strong convergence seen at batch 192."}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 9e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 3e-5","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The 9e-4 run regressed, so restore 1e-3 which matched the best regime at batch 192. Then slightly relax weight decay to 3e-5 to reduce underfitting while keeping AdamW regularization, targeting a small additional drop in validation loss."}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 192","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 224","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The step-limited setup has benefited from larger batches, but 256 overshot with coupled LR scaling. Testing an intermediate batch size of 224 keeps the strong 1e-3 setting while increasing sample coverage beyond 192, which may yield a further small loss reduction."}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 224","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"Batch-size scaling has been the strongest driver of gains in this fixed-step setting, and the previous 256 trial confounded size with a higher LR. Testing 256 at the current tuned LR/decay isolates batch effect and may further reduce validation loss."}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 224","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"Batch 256 regressed, so return to the stronger 224 regime. With that batch, a modest LR increase to 1.1e-3 tests whether slightly stronger updates can improve short-horizon convergence and push validation loss below the current best."}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"The 1.1e-3 run was slightly worse than the best 224-batch setting, so returning to 1e-3 is the highest-confidence final move to recover the strongest validated regime for this fixed-step budget."}
